entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop (Arachis hypogaea L.) studied for aflatoxin contamination in Northern Ghana

entities.name: AFLATOXIN CONTAMINATION
entities.description: The presence of aflatoxins in groundnut products, posing health risks to humans and livestock

entities.name: NORTHERN GHANA
entities.description: The geographical region in Ghana where the study on groundnut contamination was conducted

entities.name: IMPROVED PRODUCTION TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: A combination of specific varieties and agronomic practices used to reduce aflatoxin levels and increase yield

entities.name: KERNEL YIELD
entities.description: The quantity of groundnut kernels produced per unit area

entities.name: NKATIESARI
entities.description: A groundnut variety that exhibited the lowest mean aflatoxin contamination levels

entities.name: SAMNUT 22
entities.description: A groundnut variety that exhibited the highest levels of aflatoxin contamination

entities.name: EU ACCEPTABLE LIMIT
entities.description: The European Union's safety threshold for aflatoxin levels, set at 4 ppb

entities.name: HUMAN POISONING
entities.description: The toxic effect on humans resulting from the consumption of aflatoxin-contaminated groundnuts

entities.name: LIVESTOCK POISONING
entities.description: The toxic effect on animals resulting from the consumption of aflatoxin-contaminated groundnuts

relationships.description: showed the highest levels of contamination
